title: Core Kernel Standards
purpose: Cross-cutting contracts shared across kernel navigation, authored work packets, runtime apply, and standards governance itself.

kernel_navigation_hints.when: You are in codex/standards/ root or editing kernel navigation, apply, planning, or self-model code.

standards_registry.description: Global family catalog and naming contract for the standards tree.

standards_group_index.description: Per-family authority index shape used by every standards group.

artifact_ontology.description: Durable artifact families, authority hierarchy, invariants, and doctrine ordering for the entire repository.

live_projection_salience_gate.description: Cross-cutting attention-control extension for live projections: salience, workspace candidates, gate routes, action values, retrieval indexes, information scent, actor lenses, and weak/strong traces.

continuity_protocol.description: Lifecycle states, continuity modes, required yield artifacts, routing outcomes, and transition rules for session-boundary crossing.

apply_plan.description: Apply and observe runner envelope contract.

idea_packet.description: Idea-packet markdown contract for authored synthesis packets.

paper_module.description: Cold-readable subsystem ontology contract: required frontmatter, 10 required sections, compression formula (3/5/8/10 TLDR brackets by fan-in), status enum, density/dependency rules, consumer absorption brackets, frontend-card rendering contract, and metadata route coverage. Derived projections _index.json, _validation_report.json, _doctrine_to_paper_modules.json, and _route_coverage.json are mechanically regenerated by the builder. Paper-modules README.md is governed by std_markdown_doc.json; individual modules by std_paper_module.json.

imagination.description: Governed counterfactual affordance scene: a voice-anchored, dependency-honest, present-tense rendering of an unrealized or partially realized future-state, written so the deliverable's affordances become operationally obvious without pretending the state is already true. Distinct from principle (rule), axiom_candidate (compressed rule), standard (contract), paper_module (current-state explanation), and pattern_deliverable (TODO). Markdown body for the scene; YAML frontmatter for structured fields. Hard invariants: hedge-only-in-frame, voice-or-invalid, substrate-or-invalid, horizon-or-invalid, retirement-or-invalid, no-collapse-to-neighbor. Field set frozen at v1 (2026-04-29) after operator-gated review of three domain-distinct exemplars (operator_situated_morning, raw_seed_to_doctrine_factory, provider_pressure_cockpit). Adding new required fields requires a v2 schema bump.

microcosm.description: Public-safe Microcosm product/substrate primitive: the cleaner-than-macro compression of private ai_workflow laws into a smaller, runnable, low-friction object for external comprehension. Current product center is microcosm-substrate/ and `repo -> .microcosm`; release/publication authority remains external in dissemination/public gates. The old microcosm-as-experiment lane moved to the laboratory artifact and std_laboratory.json on 2026-05-21.

laboratory.description: Controlled experiment/probe substrate formerly described as microcosm: fixture-backed laboratory waves test candidate grammar, packets, routes, receipts, validators, or product mechanics before promotion through owning lanes. Evidence, not authority. Compatibility ids such as system_microcosm_probe and microcosm_autonomous_seed_plan remain until generated routes can be renamed safely.

pattern_deliverables.description: Typed substrate for problem statements / TODOs / future deliverables / paper-module overflow surfaced from inside the system. Inverse sibling of std_annex_distillation: pattern deliverables route system pressure outward toward candidate annex matches and forward toward local artifacts (one deliverable -> many candidate matches, status-routed, kind-classified). Each row is a provenance-bound, compressible, controller-reviewed problem object with status-sensitive validation: missing forward/backward routes are warnings on captured/open, errors on routed/matched/planned/landed. The v0 authority substrate, builder, and read-only kernel browse verbs are landed; the populate-deliverable mutation skill remains pending.

semantic_naming.description: Repository-wide semantic naming contract: names as compressed affordance rows, shard option surface before guessed queries, naming layers, reference-preserving repair order, repair-pattern option surface (root_invocation_shim, inline_authority_declaration, artifact_family_handle, case_and_scope_normalization), and storage-rename migration gate.

rename_migration_receipt.description: Typed proof object for storage rename migrations under the semantic naming grammar. Every storage rename must either carry a completed receipt or be accompanied by a planned/blocked receipt naming the outstanding gate precondition.

semantic_naming_audit.description: Bounded-sample naming audit receipts. Each receipt is explicitly non-exhaustive and enumerates candidate names, selected shard, faulty layer, proposed repair, reference-impact notes, and storage-rename posture. No machine standard yet; the audit receipt format lives by example at state/semantic_naming/audits/.

kind_atlas.description: Rung-0 artifact-kind atlas contract: exposes governed artifact kinds as compressed option rows before keyword routing, with honest option-surface support and profile-gap posture.

navigation_contract.description: Reusable field contract for standard-owned native navigation ladders: navigable bands, band contracts, telescope facets, source authority, currentness, dependency-neighborhood policy, and validation probes.

standard_type_plane.description: Standards-first navigation type plane: broad artifact types route first to governing standards, while the Kind Atlas, System Atlas, paper-module sidecars, and option surfaces remain projections over the standard-owned write/read contracts.

doctrine_subdomain_registry.description: Repo-template doctrine ownership lattice contract: one primary subdomain plus optional secondary tags for principles, skills, mechanisms, paper modules, and annex bundles. Frontend is the first instantiated domain at codex/doctrine/subdomains/frontend.json; builder emits _index.json and _validation_report.json.

node_tool.description: Tool-node contract for substrate runtime nodes.

orchestration_state.description: Canonical unattended-runtime ownership, append-only event log, and machine/human projections.

orchestration_events.description: Append-only JSONL of orchestration event cores when the normalized decision fingerprint changes.

reactions.description: Phase 09.35 journal-gated reactions: tracked declarative config, runtime arm/disarm state, append-only ledger, and stop-flag. Engine `tools/meta/control/reactions_engine.py`; orchestration writer projects a `reactions` block into orchestration_state.json (single-writer rule). Cold-readable map: codex/doctrine/paper_modules/reactions_engine.md.

lifecycle_surface_budget.description: Control-plane membrane contract for automatically invoked lifecycle surfaces: synchronous hooks/interceptors stay bounded, non-recursive, telemetry write-only, and elephant-state-excluding; metabolism, observability consumption, Work Ledger enrichment, host panic forensics, and large-state scans move to async or explicit lanes.

workingness_instrument.description: Candidate diagnostic truth-maintenance contract that separates observations, evidence, judgments, doctrine, projections, appeals, repairs, and mutation permissions before authority changes. Cold-readable map: codex/doctrine/paper_modules/workingness_instrument.md.

metabolism_status.description: Metabolism daemon status snapshot (schema v2) with loop-phase state machine, foreground tick timestamps, and daemon_health classifier so a stalled foreground loop cannot masquerade as healthy behind a fresh background heartbeat. Writer `tools/meta/control/metabolismd.py::MetabolismRuntime.build_status_snapshot`; foreground-tick sidecar writer `MetabolismRuntime.enter_phase`.

phase_harbor.description: Phase harbor bootstrap and deposit surface contract.

plan_note.description: Canonical implementation and execution-control note contract.

response_surfaces.description: Canonical response-surface contract for machine-authoritative outputs.

operator_action_receipt.description: Durable metadata-only receipt contract for operator-facing HUD/bridge controls: preserves claim identity and proof-bearing fields, marks missing proof explicitly, and excludes private body/debug payloads from the durable log.

constitution_workspace.description: Live what-am-I documentation global workspace rooted in the constitution seed.

typed_response_map.description: Typed response-map contract for normalized multi-surface outputs.

work_note.description: Canonical work-note and continuity packet contract.

type_a_seat_control.description: Safe provider-neutral Type A seat request contract over the Autonomy Plan/Runtime root. Attended work projects type_a_seat_dispatch approvals; overnight auto-dispatch requires explicit AFK mode plus idle proof; Claude.app is stage_only/no-submit by default; OpenRouter/NVIDIA lower-class seats run transform_job packets.

bridge_info_request.description: Candidate packet contract for bidirectional Type B to Type A evidence pull. Bridge reasoners may emit bounded information requests when exact substrate evidence blocks a named decision; Type A fulfills with evidence packets and repeated request patterns become information-demand telemetry.

provider_adapter.description: Provider adapter contract for binding LLM provider interfaces to registry rows, ledger-bound model resolution, named fallback, budget gates, rate-limit source claims, and provider receipt requirements before runtime provider refactors land.

transform_job.description: Controller-owned transform_job, row_patch, and provider_receipt contract for lower-class Type A provider workers.

station_aesthetic.description: Governing visual/interaction/layout/image-reference/verification contract for the Zenith Station cockpit. Covers design tokens (near-black #050805, muted gold #c7b06a, soft green #7bc78e, IBM Plex Sans Condensed + Mono), interaction-state rules (live/stale/paused/catching_up, muted amber/rose for warnings, 1-second feedback), layout rules (no card mosaics, operator decision support, single OrchestrationFrame driver), image-reference discipline (generated images are inspiration only — extract layout rhythm/hierarchy/state treatment, never fake data), accessibility constraints (color + secondary cue, 1/4-inch touch target), and verification contract (npm build gate + station_render screenshots). Routes via ./repo-python kernel.py --docs-route 'station aesthetic'. Paired paper module: frontend_station_cockpit.

examples_dir.role: Concrete examples and scaffold specs for standards consumers.
